places.name: Mithila
places.modernInfo: Birthplace of Sita. Janaki Mandir stands here today.

scenes.title: Sita's Swayamvar
scenes.description: King Janaka declared that whoever strings the divine bow of Shiva shall marry his daughter Sita. Many kings failed before Ram lifted and broke the bow.

scenes.title: Ram and Sita's Wedding
scenes.description: Ram and Sita were married here in a grand ceremony. His brothers also married Janaka's other daughters on the same day.

places.name: Ayodhya
places.modernInfo: Ram Mandir consecrated here in January 2024. One of the holiest cities in India.

scenes.title: Birth of Ram
scenes.description: Ram was born here to King Dasharatha and Queen Kaushalya as the seventh avatar of Lord Vishnu.

scenes.title: The Two Boons
scenes.description: Queen Kaikeyi demanded two boons from King Dasharatha — crown the prince Bharat as king and exile Ram to the forest for 14 years.

scenes.title: Ram leaves for exile
scenes.description: Ram, Sita, and Lakshman crossed the Sarayu River on a boat, beginning their 14 years of exile as the people of Ayodhya wept on the banks.

scenes.title: Ram returns to Ayodhya
scenes.description: After 14 years and defeating Ravana, Ram returned to Ayodhya. The city was lit with lamps — this day is celebrated as Diwali.

places.name: Prayagraj
places.modernInfo: Confluence of Ganga, Yamuna and Saraswati rivers. Formerly called Allahabad.

scenes.title: Crossing the Ganga
scenes.description: Ram, Sita and Lakshman crossed the sacred Ganga river here and rested at Sage Bharadwaj's ashram before moving deeper into the forest.

places.name: Chitrakoot
places.modernInfo: Sacred pilgrimage city on the Mandakini river. Home to Ramghat and many ancient temples.

scenes.title: Ram's hut on Mandakini
scenes.description: Ram built a small hut near the banks of the Mandakini river. He, Sita and Lakshman lived peacefully here for years during their exile.

scenes.title: Bharat meets Ram
scenes.description: Bharat came here to inform Ram of King Dasharatha's death and begged Ram to return. Ram refused and Bharat returned to Ayodhya with Ram's sandals to place on the throne.

scenes.title: Ram holds court in a cave
scenes.description: Ram and Lakshman held court inside this cave during their time in Chitrakoot. The cave has an underground river flowing through it.

places.name: Dandakaranya
places.modernInfo: A vast forest region covering parts of central India. Still heavily forested today.

scenes.title: Meeting Sage Agastya
scenes.description: Ram visited the great sage Agastya in the Dandaka forest. The sage gave Ram the divine bow and arrows of Brahma and Vishnu to fight the demons.

scenes.title: Surpanakha is insulted
scenes.description: Ravana's sister Surpanakha approached Ram and Lakshman. When she attacked Sita, Lakshman cut off her nose. This triggered the chain of events leading to Sita's abduction.

places.name: Panchavati
places.modernInfo: Sita Guha, Kalaram Temple and Ramkund are active pilgrimage sites here today.

scenes.title: Ram builds a home on Godavari
scenes.description: Ram, Sita and Lakshman settled here by the Godavari river. Ram built a beautiful hut among five banyan trees — Panchavati means 'five banyan trees'.

scenes.title: The Golden Deer
scenes.description: Sita was enchanted by a beautiful golden deer — actually Maricha the demon in disguise sent by Ravana. She asked Ram to capture it, luring him away from the hut.

scenes.title: Sita abducted by Ravana
scenes.description: Ravana came disguised as a sage. Sita crossed the protective line drawn by Lakshman. Ravana revealed himself, overpowered Sita and carried her away in his flying chariot Pushpaka.

places.name: Kishkindha
places.modernInfo: UNESCO World Heritage Site. Ruins of Vijayanagara Empire. Boulder-strewn landscape matches Valmiki's descriptions.

scenes.title: Ram meets Hanuman
scenes.description: Hanuman appeared before Ram and Lakshman disguised as a brahmin near the Rishyamukha hill where Sugriva was hiding from Vali.

scenes.title: Alliance with Sugriva
scenes.description: Ram and Sugriva exchanged garlands around a sacred fire, pledging mutual help — Ram would kill Vali, Sugriva would help find Sita.

scenes.title: Vali is defeated
scenes.description: Ram shot Vali with an arrow from behind a tree as Vali fought Sugriva. Dying, Vali questioned Ram. Ram explained his reason, and Vali accepted it peacefully.

scenes.title: Sugriva crowned king
scenes.description: With Vali gone, Sugriva was crowned the king of Kishkindha. He fulfilled his promise and assembled the great Vanara army to search for Sita.

scenes.title: Shabari offers berries to Ram
scenes.description: The devoted old woman Shabari had waited her entire life for Ram's arrival. She offered him berries, tasting each one first to make sure they were sweet — a symbol of pure devotion.

scenes.title: Vanara army assembled
scenes.description: Millions of Vanaras gathered at the Pampa Lake before marching south to search for Sita in all four directions of the world.

places.name: Rameshwaram
places.modernInfo: One of the 12 Jyotirlinga shrines. Ramanathaswamy Temple has the longest corridor in India.

scenes.title: Ram surveys the ocean
scenes.description: Ram climbed this hillock and surveyed the vast ocean separating India from Lanka. He prayed to the ocean god Varuna for three days to allow safe passage.

scenes.title: Building the bridge to Lanka
scenes.description: Engineer Nala and the Vanara army built a floating bridge of stones across the sea to Lanka. The stones floated because Ram's name was written on them. NASA satellite images show this formation today.

scenes.title: Ram worships Shiva
scenes.description: After defeating Ravana, Ram returned here and installed a Shiva lingam to seek forgiveness for killing Ravana — a great Shiva devotee. This temple stands here to this day.

places.name: Lanka
places.modernInfo: Sri Lanka government promotes an official Ramayana Trail tourism circuit. Sita Eliya and Ashoka Vatika are active sites.

scenes.title: Sita held captive
scenes.description: Ravana kept Sita in the beautiful Ashoka garden under guard of female demons. He tried to persuade her to marry him but she refused, holding a blade of grass between them.

scenes.title: Hanuman finds Sita
scenes.description: Hanuman leaped across the ocean to Lanka, found Sita in Ashoka Vatika, and presented Ram's ring to her as proof. He was then captured and brought before Ravana.

scenes.title: Hanuman burns Lanka
scenes.description: Ravana ordered Hanuman's tail to be set on fire. Hanuman used this to leap from building to building, burning Lanka with his own blazing tail before leaping back to India.

scenes.title: The Great Battle
scenes.description: The Vanara army crossed the bridge and fought Ravana's demon army. Great warriors fell on both sides. Lakshman was struck unconscious and Hanuman flew to the Himalayas to bring the Sanjeevani herb.

scenes.title: Ravana is defeated
scenes.description: After a fierce battle, Ram used the Brahmastra arrow given by Sage Agastya to kill Ravana. The 10-headed demon king fell, ending his reign of terror.

scenes.title: Sita's Agni Pariksha
scenes.description: To prove her purity, Sita walked through fire. The fire god Agni himself rose from the flames holding Sita unharmed, declaring her pure to the world.
